reviews.review_description: Real physics applies in early stages, but it soon demands crazy jumps and moves into fantasy. I used to enjoy the old iphone versions for adults. This vr version is only for kids, in my opinion.

This game is an immersive environment where you can design, test, and refine their engineering creations in your living room.  The mixed reality and ability to scale the model is really good.  To be honest I did have trouble understanding how and what of the game even though I followed the tutorial, but watched a quick online clip (social media) and got to understand the game, and that made it playable for me.  Not that I kept within budget for building the bridge!!   I also struggled with controls, but after some practice it became second nature.  This is fun and I can see for some players become addictive especially for fans of engineering puzzles and creative sandbox games.

I like this style of games and I bought this at launch, but stopped because I constantly have to restart a level when my controls bug out and I can no longer place roads.

Now I am back and it's still exactly the same, wish I had refunded this when I could have

developer_response.body:
Hi Lupin3rd!
We are sorry to hear about your troubles. Please send us a mail to support@clockstone.com and tell us what type of headset you are playing on (Quest 2, 3, or 3s). And if you are playing with hands or controllers. Also, a video showing the problematic behavior would be very helpful.
Best regards, the ClockStone team

reviews.review_description: Great little game, Looking forward to moving up some levels to see what changes.easy enough controls after a few goes. Fun and frustrating game but definitely more fun! Love the mixed reality side of it.

I wanted to love this like the 2d Version . Honestly it looks decent until you try to move your pieces . Depth perception is awful and the snap to point system is annoying . This is a port of a 2d game instead of building a game that is fun and intuitive. It misses the opportunity to be something worthy of VR

I really enjoyed my playtime on this game.

Controls are great and the tutorial is great too.
I’m not sure why others state about issues with controls and the tutorial, as I had none whatsoever ever.

Presentation is really nice and clean.
Sounds and graphics … and physics are very good too!

Plenty of levels to attain your brain.

You piece together your bridges going from contact point to contact point… and then place road panels down… then start your vehicle rolling… bridge collapse, then stop the vehicle, adjust your build and start the vehicle again. Very easy to play.

Mastering it though is another level, and it’s a tough challenge.. but a very fun one too… and rewarding when the vehicle reaches the other side woooooo!!!!


Highly recommend this game to puzzle players

reviews.review_title: CROSS THAT BRIDGE WHEN YOU COME TO IT
reviews.review_description:
"Whilst it can’t be denied that Bridge Constructor Studio is designed for a very specific audience, it manages to capture the same spirit of the flat games perfectly. With charming visuals and perfect VR controls, there’s not a whole lot to criticise. And with the cheap selling point, it’s definitely worth giving it a shot to see if it unlocks your inner engineer."

Read our full thoughts over at The Elite Institute (dot) net
I've been waiting for this sort of game in VR! I've completed a few games like this on 2D mobile versions. Impressed with the physics and works great. Love mixed reality mode. Defo recommend!

reviews.review_title: Another very very very good game...
reviews.review_description:
☆ - for Mixed Reality & its control
 ☆ - Concept
 ☆ - Physics,it will take some time people to adjust i am not from engineering field so cant say how real it is.but i experience just one point in the game can completely make you wrong & right.
 ☆ - Price
 ☆ - I think nothing wrong in giving one for everything combine & as it works pretty much fine.

I really enjoyed Toy Trains and the construction parts of LEGO Bricktales, so this kind of game is my jam. I wanted to take a quick peek and ended up losing an hour of my evening (that's a compliment). I've only scratched the surface, there seems like a lot to do. Really simple to learn. Graphics are bright and crisp. Challenging to stay "under budget" but very forgiving and allows you to redo things if you mess up. I can tell I'm going to kill a lot of time with this 🙂

I love the graphics and playing controls of this game. It is easy to move your playing field for comfortable play.  It is a fun concept and I enjoy planning out my bridges so they don’t come crashing down.
Fun game. A little frustrating at first until you learn the tricks of good bridge building.  Lots of entertainment for not a ton of money.

I’m definitely a huge fan of games like this.
I did struggle to get to grips with the tutorial at first, but once you get your brain around everything the game is so polished, the challenge is on from the word go.

It’s cleverly put together and you don’t need an engineering degree, kust think logically to each construction.
Driving the bus over to victory is so satisfying and worth my toil to construct the bridge .

Really looking forward to building more bridges.

The game seems fine for what it is I guess, though interactions can be more work than necessary due to the small glowing curser makes depth hard to judge and the very small  zone for items have for you to interact with them has me just floating my hand around what I need a bit longer than I'd like half the time I'm trying to activate a build spot (and sometimes getting the wrong one because 2 are right next to eachother...).
Outside of that, the game itself may just not be for me. The base mechanics I get just fine , but completing even the very first bridge under the given expense limit is utterly impossible for me.

So I may give it 1 more run for a few minutes, I really don't know if I regret the purchase or not...

For me it is a great game. I like the VR/MR UI design and I could smoothly dive into the game. The low-poly style is nice and well rounded. With MR it even blends into my living room.  :) The game is kinda relaxing but still comes with some challenges; also customized if one wants. Looking forwards for more virtual constructions.
It’s well made but the 2D is the same thing really they did not let me refund the but I think its because I played more then 2hours but ok/good game  ￼
